name: Winin Pawnbroker / Winin Pawn Shop
description_short: South Bronx pawn shop offering collateral loans, jewelry buying/selling, and pre-owned merchandise since 2011. Rated 5.0/5 from 358 Google reviews.
description_long:
Winin Pawn Broker Incorporated was incorporated in February 2010 and opened for business in March 2011, making it one of South Bronx's more established independent pawnbrokers with roughly 15 years of continuous operation. The shop is situated at 345 E 149th St in the Melrose neighborhood — a high-traffic South Bronx commercial corridor — and operates under New York State pawnbroker regulations governing loan terms, interest rates, and item handling. The business has no BBB accreditation; its BBB file, opened April 2015, carries an "Insufficient information to rate" designation, though this appears to reflect limited formal engagement with the bureau rather than a history of unresolved complaints.

Winin's core business covers three activities: issuing collateral-based pawn loans, buying items outright for cash, and retailing pre-owned merchandise. Pawn loans run on a 4-month term with extension options available, giving borrowers more runway than the 30-day windows typical of payday-style products. The shop's primary focus is jewelry — it appears in Google's business listings under Jeweler, Jewelry Appraiser, and Watch Repair Service subtypes in addition to Pawn Shop — and also handles electronics, games, DVDs, and general second-hand goods. Specific loan rates and buy/sell prices are negotiated in-store rather than published. All transactions are conducted in person at the Bronx storefront; the business has no website, no online portal, and no mobile app.

What distinguishes Winin in New York City's competitive pawnbroker landscape is its earned customer reputation. A perfect 5.0 rating aggregated from 358 Google reviews is exceptional for any retail business and particularly rare in a category that frequently draws complaints about lowball offers and high-pressure tactics. Reviewers consistently name staff members William and Alex for their professionalism, transparent dealing, and non-predatory approach to pricing. Multiple reviews specifically contrast Winin's fairness against other NYC pawn shops, suggesting the shop has built genuine goodwill in a community with limited access to traditional credit products.

Winin's principal strengths are its long operational track record, specialist focus on jewelry, and a customer reputation that stands out sharply in a segment often associated with exploitative practices. The main limitations are structural rather than reputational: there is no way to get a quote, check inventory, or transact remotely — everything requires a physical visit to 149th Street in the South Bronx. Pricing is not published, which demands comparison shopping in person. The single location also limits reach for NYC residents outside the immediate area. For Bronx residents who can walk in, however, Winin represents a community-trusted option with an unusually strong track record.
pros: Perfect 5.0 Google rating from 358 reviews — exceptional for a neighborhood pawnbroker in NYC
15+ years in continuous operation since March 2011, with deep roots in the South Bronx community
Named staff (William and Alex) repeatedly cited for professionalism, fairness, and non-predatory pricing
4-month pawn loan terms with extension options — significantly longer runway than typical 30-day payday products
Multi-specialty operation listed under Pawn Shop, Jeweler, Jewelry Appraiser, and Watch Repair Service on Google
Located in high-traffic Melrose commercial corridor with easy walk-in access for South Bronx residents
cons: No website, online portal, or mobile app — all transactions require an in-person visit to the South Bronx storefront
Loan rates and buy/sell prices are not published publicly; customers must visit in-store to receive a quote
BBB file (opened April 2015) carries an 'Insufficient information to rate' status — no formal BBB rating issued
Single location limits accessibility for NYC residents outside the immediate Bronx area
best_for: South Bronx and Bronx residents needing quick cash against jewelry or other valuables
Buyers seeking affordable pre-owned jewelry, electronics, or general merchandise in the NYC area
NYC residents without access to traditional credit who need a collateral-based cash loan
Sellers looking to liquidate jewelry or valuables quickly for cash without auction delays
diagnosis: Winin is best suited for Bronx residents who need fast cash against collateral — particularly jewelry — and want a community-trusted shop with a documented reputation for fair dealing. The primary caveat is that the business operates entirely in person with no online presence, making it inaccessible to anyone who cannot physically travel to 345 E 149th St.
services: Collateral-based pawn loans (4-month terms with extension options)
Games and DVDs buying and selling
